target_notes: Vesuvius Challenge — fragment 47 (Frag1 in scrollprize.org's organization) of the Herculaneum scroll P.Herc.Paris.2 from the Bibliothèque Nationale de France (the Paris fragments). Scanned at the Diamond Light Source (UK) at 54 keV X-ray energy and 3.24 micron voxel size. Persisted level 5 of the OME-NGFF multiscale Zarr pyramid (most-downsampled, ~3.4 MB). Source: scrollprize.org / dl.ash2txt.org. The fragment is one of nine published carbonized papyrus pieces; PHercParis2Fr47 was used as competition data for the Vesuvius Challenge first letters prize.
capture_method: Walked the autoindex listings under https://dl.ash2txt.org/fragments/Frag1/ to locate the OME-NGFF Zarr root. Downloaded the root .zattrs (multiscales metadata, 3 KB) + .zgroup, then level 5/.zarray + 4 blosc/zstd-compressed chunks (chunks 0/0/0, 0/0/1, 1/0/0, 1/0/1, totaling ~3.4 MB compressed). Levels 0-4 (full-res through 16x downsampled) NOT downloaded — those would balloon to GBs. zarr v3.1 reads the v2-format store via zarr.open(mode='r'). Validated voxel range 20983-32657 (uint16; consistent with X-ray CT attenuation values for carbonized organic material).
